name: Show the Sequence
description:
Description
───────────
The problem of finding the next term of a given sequence of numbers is usually proposed in QI tests. We want to generate the N terms of a sequence from a given codification of the sequence.

Let S = (Si)i denote a sequence of real numbers whose i -order term is Si . We codify a constant sequence with the following operator:

S = [n] meaning that Si = n € ,

where n € . We also define the following operators on a given sequence of numbers S = (Si)i € :

1$ \cr}; \end{displaymath} -->

V = [m + S] meaning that Vi = ;

1$ \cr}; \end{displaymath} -->

V = [m * S] meaning that Vi = ;

where m € . For example we have the following codifications:

[2 + [1]] = 2, 3, 4, 5, 6 ...
[1 + [2 + [1]]] = 1, 3, 6, 10, 15, 21, 28, 36 ...

[2 * [1 + [2 + [1]]]] = 2, 6, 36, 360, 5400, 113400 ...

[2 * [5 + [- 2]]] = 10, 30, 30, -30, 90, -450, 3150 ...

Given a codification, the problem is to write the first N terms of the sequence.

Input
─────
The input file contains several test cases. For each of them, the program input is a single line containing the codification, without any space, followed by an integer N (250) .

Output
──────
For each test case, the program output is a single line containing the list of first N terms of the sequence.

Sample Input
────────────
    [2+[1]] 3
    [2*[5+[-2]]] 7

Sample Output
─────────────
    2 3 4
    10 30 30 -30 90 -450 3150

Keyword:
